page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd is a high-density, memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 288 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors, utilizing 144 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is configured with 2232 GB of RAM, providing approximately 7.75 GB of memory per core. For local storage, the instance bundles 48 local NVMe SSDs, delivering a total capacity of 19031 GB.

In benchmark evaluations, this instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and PassMark CPU metrics. Memory subsystem performance is exceptionally strong, with read-only cached memory bandwidth reaching 22962.94 GB/sec and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 1060.25 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks also rank in the top 10%, reflecting high throughput capabilities. However, while prompt processing for large language models is strong, text generation speeds for smaller models scale poorly, landing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-288-lssd offers a highly consolidated resource profile, combining massive compute density, substantial memory, and extensive local NVMe storage on a single node. This reduces the need for distributed clustering for large datasets, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning training workloads.

This server is highly suited for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, in-memory data grids like Redis, high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd is a high-density, memory-optimized server featuring 288 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 2232 GB of RAM and 19031 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 48 drives. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database operation speeds. It is highly optimized for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and high-throughput data processing. While it lacks GPU acceleration, its massive physical core count and extensive local storage make it an efficient single-node solution for enterprise-grade applications, in-memory caching, and complex software compilation tasks.

meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd features 288 vCPUs, 2232 GB RAM, and 19 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized platform combining massive multi-core compute power with extensive local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.

2232 GB of RAM optimized for memory-intensive applications
19031 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 48 drives
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis, and software compilation
